calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Continued To Sep-08-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Henry Alvarez, Iii'S Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Dec-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Henry Alvarez, Iii'S Motion To Change Venue is denied. In his memorandum of points and authorities, Defendant moves for change of venue pursuant to CCP 392. It is apparent that Defendant means CCP 397(b) ("When there is reason to believe that an impartial trial cannot be had therein.") Defendant has not met his burden of showing that a change of venue is warranted. (See Nguyen v. Superior Court (1996) 49 Cal. App. 4th 1781, 1791.) The anecdotal evidence in the form of a limited number of news articles -- the most recent of which is from May 2013 - are insufficient to show that defendant cannot receive an impartial jury trial. Defendant's characterization of a handful of articles as "viciously negative" and an "editorial pogrom" is not sufficient to demonstrate a "widespread feeling of prejudice extending over a long time." (Nguyen, supra, at 1791.) The statistical evidence, in the form of an on-line survey, is also insufficient. The data is not a random sample, it asked leading questions, and there is no way to determine whether the respondents were a representative sample of San Francisco jurors. Further, the on-line survey failed to ask whether the respondents could be fair and impartial jurors. The Court credits the declaration of Ben Tulchin in opposition to the motion to transfer, which sets forth the methodological flaws in defendant's survey evidence. Further, even if the evidence in the on-line survey were meaningful (and the Court finds on balance that it is not), the data is subject to different interpretations and conclusions. Defendant's polling expert says "40% of those polled had seen or heard some media coverage of the SFHA prior to the poll." (Bradley Declaration in support of Motion, 7.) That means that 60% had not. Finally, the experience in the recent Myers trial, as anecdotally relayed by Defendant, does not suggest that Defendant could not have an impartial trial in this County. It is a commonplace to instruct jurors not to read any media coverage, whether in print or on-line, and to take steps to ensure that this order is complied with during trial if circumstances suggest that the order might have been violated. Overall, considering all of the evidence, Defendant has not shown that he could not have an impartial trial in San Francisco in 2014. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Defendant Henry Alvarez, Iii'S Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Continued To Dec-04-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Change Venue
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HENRY ALVAREZ, III Motion To Change Venue IS Continued to November 27, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 20, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the moving papers or the supplemental declarations filed on November 12, 2013, none of which are scanned on the Court's register of actions. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Set For Trial On Apr-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEVIN B. CLUNE, ESQ., 415.371.8500, FOR PLAINTIFF TIM LARSEN; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HENRY ALVAREZ' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a); the notice of motion does not "quote in full the portion sought to be stricken except where the motion is to strike an entire paragraph. . . ." In any event, Defendant has not established, on the face of the Complaint or from any judicially noticed matter, that the allegations (referred to by paragraph, page and line number) are subject to the attorney-client privilege. Plaintiff's allegations support a claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar. Dismissal of all claims against CCSF filed on March 1, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
